title: Raibania Fort
tagline: Raibania Fort — heritage city of Odisha

overview.short: Raibania fort is a group of ancient forts in Baleswar district, Odisha, India.
overview.description: Raibania fort is a group of ancient forts in Baleswar district, Odisha, India. This fort complex is considered as the biggest medieval fort of eastern India. It was built during the reign of Eastern Ganga ruler Narasingha Deva I. There are 161 fort goddesses which are as "Durga devata" or "Iṣṫa devata" and Daṣasharu which is an aniconic form of the goddess Jayachaṇḍi. Although three forts were recorded in the Ain-i-Akbari, four forts have been found here: two of the larger ones are closer to the village Raibania, and the other two are closer to the village Phulta (Phulahatta).

topPlaces.name: Behera Sahi
topPlaces.description: Behera Sahi, also written Beherasahi, is a village located near Jaleswar, Balasore district on the bank of Subarnarekha River. It has a population of 1460 on account of the census 2011.

topPlaces.name: Dantan I
topPlaces.description: Dantan I is a community development block that forms an administrative division in the Kharagpur subdivision of Paschim Medinipur district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Dantan
topPlaces.description: Dantan (Bengali pronunciation: [dãtɔn]) is a village in the Dantan I CD block in the Kharagpur subdivision of the Paschim Medinipur district in the state of West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Nekurseni
topPlaces.description: Nekurseni is a small town in Paschim Medinipur District of West Bengal, India located approximately 50 km south of Kharagpur. Nekurseni is an ancient town dating back to the era of Ashoka.

items.note: Behera Sahi, also written Beherasahi, is a village located near Jaleswar, Balasore district on the bank of Subarnarekha River. It has a population of 1460 on account of the census 2011.
Dantan I is a community development block that forms an administrative division in the Kharagpur subdivision of Paschim Medinipur district in the Indian state of West Bengal.
Dantan (Bengali pronunciation: [dãtɔn]) is a village in the Dantan I CD block in the Kharagpur subdivision of the Paschim Medinipur district in the state of West Bengal, India.
Nekurseni is a small town in Paschim Medinipur District of West Bengal, India located approximately 50 km south of Kharagpur. Nekurseni is an ancient town dating back to the era of Ashoka.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Raibania Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Raibania Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Raibania Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International Airport, Kolkata (153 km) and the nearest railway station is Howrah Junction (Kolkata) (140 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Raibania Fort?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Raibania Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Raibania Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Raibania Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Raibania Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Behera Sahi, Dantan I, Dantan, Nekurseni.
